title: Sokołowski's Conjecture (Polynomial Neighborhood Complexity of Planar Graphs)
statement_text: There exists a constant $c$ such that $|\{N^{r}[v]\cap A\mid v\in V(G)\}|=\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{c}|A|)$ for every planar graph $G$, every nonempty subset $A$ of vertices, and every nonnegative integer $r$.
context_text: Sokołowski conjectured that the neighborhood complexity of planar graphs is polynomial, following his earlier result showing a bound of $\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{7}|A|^{3})$, which achieved polynomial dependence on $r$ at the cost of a cubic dependence on $|A|$. The general bound given by Reidl, Sánchez Villaamil and Stravopoulos is exponential, so a polynomial bound for specific classes would be a significant improvement.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full: the neighborhood complexity of planar graphs is in $\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{4})$, and moreover the strictly stronger profile complexity of planar graphs (and all proper minor-closed classes) is also in $\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{4})$.
notes: Abstract cites the conjecture as 'Sokołowski (2021)'; the bibliography entry [28] lists the published version as Electronic Journal of Combinatorics, 30(2):P2.3, 2023, suggesting the conjecture appeared in an earlier preprint.

title: Problem 41
statement_text: Determine the profile and neighborhood complexities of planar graphs up to a constant factor. For profile complexity, it is known to be in $\Omega(r^{3})$ (Theorem 36) and in $\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{4})$ (Theorem 2). For neighborhood complexity, it is known to be in $\Omega(r^{2})$ (Theorem 36 and Lemma 8) and in $\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(r^{4})$ (Theorem 2).
context_text: The paper closes with two open problems. The first asks for tight bounds on the profile and neighborhood complexities of planar graphs; the existing lower bounds ($\Omega(r^3)$ for profile, $\Omega(r^2)$ for neighborhood) and the paper's new upper bound ($\mathcal{O}(r^4)$ for both) leave a small gap that the authors consider tantalizingly close.

title: Problem 42
statement_text: Are the profile and neighborhood complexities of $K_{t}$-minor-free graphs in $r^{\operatorname{\mathcal{O}}(t)}$?
context_text: The second open problem asks whether the polynomial bound for proper minor-closed classes can be sharpened so that the degree of the polynomial grows only logarithmically (or polynomially) in the size $t$ of the excluded complete minor, rather than being a fixed polynomial independent of $t$.
